element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental traversal; player learns momentum and platforming
Stationary speed boost mechanic; enables puzzle solutions and shortcuts
Cooperative presence; can carry player over gaps but introduces learning curve
Velocity preservation and trajectory control; core to level pacing
Momentum-based navigation; requires understanding of speed thresholds
Breath timer mechanic; adds environmental pressure distinct from spikes
Spin-jump combat; curling into ball destroys enemies
Temporary protection layers; collectible from item boxes
Core resource management; scattered on hit, blinking disappear mechanic
Progress safeguard; enables recovery from mistakes

element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of each zone (Act 3 or Act 2)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Pattern memorization; telegraphed attacks; culmination of zone mechanics
Optional Chaos Emerald collection; distinct gameplay (rotation/perspective shift)
Pacing pressure; game over condition if exceeded
Life extension at 100 rings or via item pickups

typical_tension_triggers: Loss of rings mid-air over hazard
Time limit pressure (final 30 seconds audible cue)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High-speed momentum-based with alternating safe traversal and hazard gauntlets; larger levels enable extended flow periods punctuated by spike/pit challenges; Act 1 acts as zone introduction, Act 2 escalates complexity and introduces zone-specific hazards, Boss acts force deceleration and pattern recognition

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Alternate paths visible but require specific momentum/positioning or spin dash activation; upper routes often lead to rings and power-ups; lower routes shortcut but risk hazards
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Blocks and walls positioned to suggest hidden areas; player must use spring momentum or crouch under low ceilings; some routes only accessible via speed threshold crossing (requires spin dash or downslope acceleration)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Emerald Hill Zone introduces core mechanics (running, jumping, spin dash, Tails) in low-pressure environment with abundant rings and forgiving spacing; Act 1 focuses on speed control and spring mechanics; Act 2 adds vertical loops and light platforming
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Aquatic Ruin and subsequent zones layer hazards: water drowning timers, spike fields, narrower platforms; introduces pattern-based enemy encounters; boss difficulty escalates requiring 2-4 hits; Tails AI inconsistency becomes liability in complex sections
difficulty_curve.late_game: Increasing spike density, longer bottomless pit sections, faster enemy patterns; boss encounters require frame-perfect timing; time limit pressure increases as act complexity grows; optional special stages introduce completely different control paradigm (3D space rotation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequence of angled springs launching player upward/forward; requires momentum preservation and landing precision; used to bypass lower pit sections or reach upper optional paths
Connected loop structures requiring maintained speed threshold; failure to enter with sufficient velocity causes fall-through; success provides momentum carry-forward
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Emerald Hill Zone Act 2, Jungle Zone

notable_level_patterns.description: Submerged or surface-skimming sections with audible countdown beep; requires awareness of bubble spawn locations; combines platform precision with time pressure
Narrow passages flanked by spike walls or ceilings; requires precise control deceleration and platform landing; spike collision instant-kill without shield
Confined horizontal space where Robotnik deploys themed attack pattern; player must dodge projectiles/hazards and respond with spin-jump attacks; 2-4 hits typical
Section requiring specific speed threshold to reach collectible or progress; spin dash enables multiple solution approaches; signals player agency in path selection

notes: Sources tcrf.net and data_crystal.tcrf.net contained insufficient gameplay design details; primary game structure, level count, and mechanic introductions extracted from Wikipedia Gameplay and Development sections. Level introduction order inferred from canonical zone sequence. Tension/relief and spatial grammar patterns derived from documented level design features (springs, loops, hazards, bosses, water mechanics). Exact timing data not available in provided sources.
